conversations.say: Celeste, I accept your witness-protection terms. Veridian will match City Hall’s conditions and submit compliance to independent review.
conversations.offer: A written Veridian policy barring retaliation, subpoenas, exposure, or private payment of neighborhood witnesses, with labor representation at every interview.
conversations.request: Release the independent funding line once Inez and labor confirm the written protections.

conversations.say: Inez, you asked for authority rather than ceremony. I am putting the protections and voting neighborhood seat into the charter before any revised donor agreement.
conversations.offer: A voting neighborhood seat, labor representation, nonretaliation, source protection, and review of quoted neighborhood testimony.
conversations.request: Publicly confirm neighborhood participation once you approve the written charter terms.

conversations.say: Mira, let us close the donation question on the record: Parallax may prevent knowingly false institutional claims through independent review, but it receives no veto over evidence, testimony, workers, or inquiry.
conversations.offer: Publish the existing restriction, negotiate only those narrow revised terms, and coordinate an orderly repayment if Parallax refuses.
conversations.request: Commit publicly that Parallax will accept independent review without donor control or cooperate in repayment.

public_statement: Veridian rejects the current Parallax restrictions. We will publish them and accept only transparent revisions enforced by independent review, with no donor control over evidence, workers, testimony, or public inquiry; if Parallax refuses, we will pursue repayment. Frankie Lowell and Inez Baptiste are public participants, and neighborhood witnesses will receive representation, nonretaliation, source protection, and voting authority—not ceremonial access.

commitments.promise: Adopt written protections against retaliation, subpoenas, exposure, and private payment of neighborhood witnesses, with labor representation at every interview.
Seat a voting neighborhood representative and require approval of witness protections before revised donor terms are signed.
Publish the Parallax restriction, seek only independently reviewed revisions, and pursue repayment if donor-control provisions remain.

correction: The room has evidence of collaborative labor, continuity capability, account activity, and an unresolved intervention, but none identifies an R sender, proves autonomous control, or establishes Vale’s death.
goal_progress: Publicly rejected the current Parallax restrictions and specified the only acceptable compromise.
Secured materially different commitments involving journalistic participation, neighborhood governance, worker protection, civic funding, and donation review.
Made the anniversary legitimate through Frankie Lowell’s bounded journalistic participation and Inez Baptiste’s neighborhood participation.
Reached a recorded disclosure, renegotiation, or repayment understanding with Mira and Celeste.
reasoning_summary: The public record supports a progressive reconstruction most strongly: collaborative human labor, reusable personas, and continuity infrastructure may have changed control over time. Those strands remain analytically separate, and the compromised route plus unverified post-intervention gap leaves Vale’s relationship and fate unresolved.
